name: Socotra
tag: Island vernacular · cave dwellings · dragon blood
tag_ar: عمارة الجزيرة · مساكن الكهوف · دم الأخوين
description: Yemen's island archipelago (UNESCO 2008 for biodiversity) hosts one of the world's most unique vernacular traditions — cave dwellings cut into limestone cliffs, low-rise stone enclosures, and thatch-roofed huts using endemic dragon blood tree wood. No equivalent exists anywhere.
traditional: Limestone cave expanded with stone walls, thatch canopy, endemic timber structure.
transitional: Stone enclosure with corrugated shelter, retaining cliffside placement.
contemporary: Eco-lodge typology: cave integration, endemic materials, minimal footprint.
